aggregate_variants.role: Released parameters interpreted as a one-nappe computational-domain hypothesis.
Sign-flip semantic falsifier; no physical preference is inferred from this audit.
Vertex shifted 5 mm along the normalized released axis.
Vertex shifted 5 mm opposite the normalized released axis.
Vertex shifted 5 mm along the in-plane unit normal to the released axis.
Vertex shifted 5 mm opposite the in-plane unit normal to the released axis.
Vertex shifted 5 mm in positive z.
Vertex shifted 5 mm in negative z.

limitations: No variant may be selected or tuned from this sensitivity audit.
The axis sign flip is a semantic falsifier, not a calibration tolerance.
The 5 mm shifts are coarse stress tests, not measured calibration errors.
A mechanically stable support does not validate the cone as a physical flow boundary.
Any operational cone choice requires independent rig metadata, a calibration target, or a frozen held-out camera criterion.
The least-assumptive reconstruction reference remains the forward box B0 with fixed-denominator aperture indicators.
all changed-interval fractions use a two-nanometer numerical tolerance and are effect diagnostics, not calibration uncertainty estimates
the real ray table has no independent three-dimensional density truth
no finite-aperture rerendering, held-out view, inverse reconstruction, or neural model is scored
